signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Shock + Carry + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 157.45 — Strait of Hormuz closure fears drive WTI above $99.50, supporting USD via energy-security flows while JPY remains pressured by the 300bp Fed-BoJ carry deficit. Despite hawkish BoJ Summary of Opinions and US-Japan currency coordination talks, JPY safe-haven function is broken (USD/JPY vs US 10Y corr z=-1.92σ). Carry dominates until VIX > 25; however, intervention risk rises on any test of 160.00.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7222 — COT LF net long +58,994 (78.3% long) vs retail 89% short. Institutional vs retail divergence is at an extreme as specs chase the RBA 4.35% carry advantage (-60bp vs Fed). Retail capitulation risk is elevated if price extends higher, though crowded spec longs suggest late-trend caution. Watch for RBA meeting on 16 Jun to confirm hiking bias.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -19,294 (long% 18.5%) while RBNZ maintains a hawkish hike bias for the 27 May meeting. Specs are fading the central bank despite a 150bp carry disadvantage for NZD vs USD. If RBNZ signals a near-term hike, the crowded spec short position faces a significant unwind/squeeze risk. NZD/USD at 0.5950.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + US CPI Risk
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1759 — Correlation with EuroStoxx has flipped to +0.701 (z=+1.87σ vs norm -0.125), indicating EUR is trading as a pure risk-proxy rather than on idiosyncratic fundamentals. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% (175bp spread) structurally limits upside. Market is pricing 'hot' US CPI risk which would reinforce the USD carry advantage. Watch 1.1700 support on CPI release.
